items.title: Operation Dragon Weave: Uncovering a China-Linked Campaign Targeting Czech Republic and Taiwan Using Azure Cloud C2
items.description: A sophisticated cyber-espionage campaign attributed to China-linked actors targets officials and citizens in Czech Republic and Taiwan through spearphishing attacks. The operation deploys malicious ZIP archives containing dual infection paths that ultimately deliver AZUREVEIL, an Adaptix C2 agent. The campaign uniquely leverages Microsoft Azure Blob Storage as a dead-drop command-and-control chann
items.ai_summary: The Operation Dragon Weave campaign, attributed to China-linked actors, utilizes spearphishing attacks to target officials and citizens in the Czech Republic and Taiwan. The attackers deploy malicious ZIP archives containing dual infection paths, which ultimately deliver the AZUREVEIL malware, an Adaptix C2 agent. If exploited, this campaign can lead to data encryption and exfiltration, with real-world impact including compromised sensitive information and potential disruption of government services. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering, ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches, and monitor for suspicious Azure Blob Storage activity. Specifically, defenders should configure their email gateways to block ZIP archives with suspicious attachments and implement a security information and event management (SIEM) system to monitor for T1071 Application Layer Protocol anomalies.

graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1560.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Campaign Hits Red Hat Cloud Services npm Packages
items.description: A supply chain attack compromised multiple @redhat-cloud-services npm packages, executing malicious payloads automatically during installation via preinstall hooks. The attack uses AES-GCM encrypted payloads and obfuscated JavaScript loaders to harvest GitHub Actions secrets, npm tokens, cloud credentials (AWS, Azure, GCP), Kubernetes and Vault material, SSH keys, Git credentials, and cryptocurren
items.ai_summary: The Mini Shai-Hulud campaign is a supply chain attack that compromised multiple @redhat-cloud-services npm packages, allowing attackers to execute malicious payloads during installation via preinstall hooks. The attack uses AES-GCM encrypted payloads and obfuscated JavaScript loaders to harvest sensitive information such as GitHub Actions secrets, npm tokens, cloud credentials, Kubernetes and Vault material, SSH keys, Git credentials, and cryptocurrency. The affected packages are part of Red Hat Cloud Services, and users who have installed these packages are at risk of having their sensitive information stolen. To remediate this issue, defenders should immediately remove the compromised packages and update to the latest versions, and also rotate all sensitive credentials, including GitHub Actions secrets, npm tokens, and cloud credentials. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for any suspicious activity and implement additional security measures such as multi-factor authentication and encryption to protect their sensitive information.

graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1078.004| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1027| D[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Impact]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: FSB’s matryoshka #1/3 – Gamaredon’s gifts that keeps unpacking – GammaPhish and GammaWorm
items.description: Gamaredon, a cyberespionage group operated by Russia's FSB, conducts long-term intrusion operations targeting Ukrainian government, military, and critical infrastructure. This analysis documents their 2026 infection chain, which uses HTML smuggling with weaponized xHTML files delivering RAR archives that exploit CVE-2025-8088 to extract HTA files into Windows Startup directories. The chain deploys
items.ai_summary: The Gamaredon group, operated by Russia's FSB, has been conducting long-term intrusion operations targeting Ukrainian government, military, and critical infrastructure entities. The infection chain utilizes HTML smuggling with weaponized xHTML files, delivering RAR archives that exploit a vulnerability to extract HTA files into Windows Startup directories. If exploited, this vulnerability allows attackers to gain persistence and potentially exfiltrate sensitive data, with real-world impact including compromised government and military systems. To remediate, defenders should update their systems to the latest version of Windows and ensure that all startup directories are properly secured, and monitor for suspicious HTA file executions. Additionally, implementing a robust email filtering solution to block malicious xHTML files and RAR archives can help prevent initial access.

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: A New Threat Actor Using ClickFix and Fake Update Drive-By Attacks in Thousands of Compromised Sites
items.description: A sophisticated threat actor named DriveSurge operates as an Initial Access Broker using a Pay-Per-Install model to deliver malware at scale. The actor compromises thousands of legitimate websites and uses zTDS (Traffic Distribution System) to silently redirect visitors to malicious content. Victims encounter either FakeUpdates campaigns that impersonate browser update prompts for 11 different bro
items.ai_summary: The DriveSurge threat actor utilizes a Pay-Per-Install model to deliver malware at scale, compromising thousands of legitimate websites and leveraging zTDS to silently redirect visitors to malicious content. This campaign employs drive-by attacks, specifically T1189, to infect victims' systems, often through FakeUpdates campaigns that mimic browser update prompts for 11 different browsers. If exploited, victims may experience unauthorized access and potential data breaches, with real-world impact seen in compromised websites and infected user systems. To remediate, defenders should implement robust web application firewalls, ensure all software is up-to-date, and utilize browser extensions that detect and block malicious scripts, such as uBlock Origin or NoScript. Additionally, network traffic monitoring for suspicious zTDS activity and regular security audits can help identify potential vulnerabilities.

    B -->|T1189| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1566| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Malicious npm packages abuse dependency confusion to profile developer environments
items.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence identified an active supply chain attack involving malicious npm packages that employ dependency confusion techniques. Between May 28-29, 2026, a threat actor using three maintainer aliases published malicious packages across nine organizational scopes that mirror real corporate namespaces. The packages execute obfuscated reconnaissance payloads through npm lifecycle
items.ai_summary: The malicious npm packages exploit dependency confusion to profile developer environments, specifically targeting npm packages with similar names to legitimate corporate namespaces. The attack vector involves publishing malicious packages across nine organizational scopes, executing obfuscated reconnaissance payloads through npm lifecycle hooks. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, with potential real-world impact on organizations that rely on npm packages, such as software development companies. To remediate, defenders should verify the authenticity of npm packages, ensuring they are installed from trusted sources, and monitor for suspicious activity, such as unusual network traffic or system changes. Additionally, developers should implement secure dependency management practices, including regular package audits and updates, to prevent similar attacks.

    C -->|T1059.001| D[Persistence]:::tactic

items.title: Nimbus RAT: How Threat Actors Are Abusing Microsoft Teams and Google Drive to Deploy a Java RAT
items.description: In April 2026, threat actors deployed Nimbus RAT against a legal industry target using Microsoft Teams voice phishing. The attack began with email bombing (282 emails in 90 minutes), followed by a fake IT helpdesk contact via Teams who convinced the victim to grant Quick Assist remote access. Within 20 minutes, a Java-based RAT was deployed that uses Google Drive and Google Sheets for command-and-
items.ai_summary: The Nimbus RAT threat involves a sophisticated phishing campaign targeting the legal industry, where threat actors use Microsoft Teams voice phishing to trick victims into granting remote access via Quick Assist. The attack begins with a barrage of emails, followed by a fake IT helpdesk contact that convinces the victim to provide access. Once access is gained, a Java-based RAT is deployed, utilizing Google Drive and Google Sheets for command and control. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement multi-factor authentication for remote access tools, restrict access to sensitive data, and educate users about the risks of voice phishing and the importance of verifying the identity of IT helpdesk personnel. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity related to Google Drive and Google Sheets, such as unusual file uploads or access requests.

    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold

items.title: From Token Bingo to MAX Takeover: Kali365 Operator Expands Operation Across Microsoft Outlook, Okta, Xerox DocuShare, and Other Services
items.description: A significant expansion of the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service operation has been observed, now targeting multiple platforms beyond Microsoft 365. The operator abuses OAuth 2.0 device authorization flows to bypass MFA and steal authentication tokens. Key discoveries include a live command-and-control panel infrastructure, a phishing campaign impersonating MAX Messenger (Russia's state-backed messagi
items.ai_summary: The Kali365 phishing-as-a-service operation has expanded to target multiple platforms, including Microsoft Outlook, Okta, and Xerox DocuShare, by abusing OAuth 2.0 device authorization flows to bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA) and steal authentication tokens. This attack vector, identified as T1566 (Phishing), allows the threat actors to gain initial access to the targeted systems. The real-world impact of this exploit includes the potential for unauthorized access to sensitive information and systems, affecting organizations that rely on these services. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement specific security measures, such as verifying the authenticity of OAuth 2.0 authorization flows and monitoring for suspicious activity. Additionally, organizations should ensure that their MFA solutions are properly configured to prevent bypass attempts, such as requiring additional verification steps for high-risk transactions.

    D -->|T1059.001| E[Command and Control]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Iran Expands Handala Brand to Physical Threats
items.description: Iran's Ministry of Intelligence has broadened its Handala brand beyond cyber operations to include physical threats and influence campaigns targeting US and Israeli interests. The expansion encompasses multiple personas: Handala Popular Resistance Front claiming physical attacks inside Israel, VIPEmployment recruiting proxies globally for espionage and sabotage, and MOISIRAN conducting surveillanc
items.ai_summary: The Iranian Ministry of Intelligence has expanded its Handala brand to incorporate physical threats and influence campaigns, targeting US and Israeli interests. This expansion involves multiple personas, including the Handala Popular Resistance Front, which claims responsibility for physical attacks within Israel, and VIPEmployment, a recruiter of global proxies for espionage and sabotage. The real-world impact of this expansion could result in increased physical attacks and espionage activities against US and Israeli interests, potentially affecting government agencies, private companies, and individuals. To remediate this threat, defenders should enhance their physical security measures, implement robust surveillance systems, and conduct thorough background checks on employees and contractors. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activities and report any potential threats to the relevant authorities.

items.title: Operation FlutterBridge: macOS Malvertising Campaign Spreads New FlutterShell Backdoor
items.description: A financially-motivated cybercrime cluster designated CL-CRI-1089 has launched Operation FlutterBridge, deploying FlutterShell backdoor malware targeting macOS systems through malvertising. Built with the Flutter framework, FlutterShell masquerades as legitimate applications including podcast players and PDF viewers, delivering adware with full backdoor capabilities such as shell command execution
items.ai_summary: Operation FlutterBridge is a malvertising campaign launched by the financially-motivated cybercrime cluster CL-CRI-1089, targeting macOS systems with the FlutterShell backdoor malware. The malware is built using the Flutter framework and disguises itself as legitimate applications, such as podcast players and PDF viewers, to deliver adware with full backdoor capabilities, including shell command execution. If exploited, this vulnerability allows threat actors to gain remote access to compromised systems, potentially leading to data breaches, ransomware attacks, or other malicious activities. To remediate this threat, defenders should block suspicious traffic related to malvertising, implement robust ad-blocking mechanisms, and regularly scan systems for signs of the FlutterShell backdoor. Additionally, users should be cautious when installing applications from untrusted sources and verify the legitimacy of software before installation.

items.title: Phoenix Rising: Exposing the PhaaS Kit Behind Global Mass Phishing Campaigns
items.description: Since January 2025, researchers identified over 2,500 phishing domains targeting more than 70 organizations across financial services, telecommunications, and logistics sectors globally. Two dominant smishing campaigns were discovered: Reward Points phishing impersonating banks and telecom providers, and Failed Parcel Delivery phishing mimicking logistics companies. Despite different themes, both
items.ai_summary: The Phoenix Rising phishing campaign has been identified as a significant threat, with over 2,500 phishing domains targeting more than 70 organizations globally since January 2025. The campaign utilizes two dominant smishing themes: Reward Points phishing, which impersonates banks and telecom providers, and Failed Parcel Delivery phishing, which mimics logistics companies. The attack vector involves exploiting public-facing applications, as indicated by the detected TTP T1190, and phishing, as indicated by T1566. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust email filtering and verification processes, such as DMARC, SPF, and DKIM, and educate users on identifying and reporting suspicious emails. Additionally, organizations should conduct regular security audits to identify and address vulnerabilities in their public-facing applications.

items.title: CVE-2026-9400: A flaw has been found in Edimax BR-6675nD 1.12. This issue affects the function ...
items.description: A flaw has been found in Edimax BR-6675nD 1.12. This issue affects the function formUSBStorage of the file /goform/formUSBStorage of the component POST Request Handler. Executing a manipulation of the argument sub_dir can lead to command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9400 is a command injection vulnerability in the Edimax BR-6675nD 1.12 router, specifically in the formUSBStorage function of the /goform/formUSBStorage file, which is part of the POST Request Handler component. An attacker can exploit this issue by manipulating the sub_dir argument, allowing for remote command injection. If successfully exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access and control of the affected device, potentially resulting in data breaches or disruption of network services. To remediate this issue, users should update their Edimax BR-6675nD router to a version later than 1.12, and ensure that any remote access to the device is properly secured. Additionally, network administrators should monitor for suspicious activity and consider implementing network segmentation to limit the spread of potential attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-48832: action/cookie.php in ecrire in SPIP before 4.4.15 is prone to an open redirect v...
items.description: action/cookie.php in ecrire in SPIP before 4.4.15 is prone to an open redirect vulnerability.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-48832 is an open redirect vulnerability in the action/cookie.php file of the ecrire module in SPIP versions prior to 4.4.15. This vulnerability allows an attacker to redirect users to arbitrary websites, potentially leading to phishing attacks or other malicious activities. The attack vector involves tricking a user into clicking a specially crafted link, which then redirects them to a malicious site. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their SPIP installations to version 4.4.15 or later, and consider implementing additional security measures such as input validation and output encoding to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future. Users of affected versions should prioritize this update to prevent potential phishing attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-9399: A vulnerability was detected in Edimax BR-6675nD 1.12. This vulnerability affect...
items.description: A vulnerability was detected in Edimax BR-6675nD 1.12. This vulnerability affects the function formsetPPPoE of the file /goform/formsetPPPoE of the component POST Request Handler. Performing a manipulation of the argument pppUserName results in buffer overflow. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this discl
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9399 is a buffer overflow vulnerability in the Edimax BR-6675nD 1.12 router, specifically in the formsetPPPoE function of the /goform/formsetPPPoE file, which can be exploited via a manipulated pppUserName argument in a POST request. This vulnerability can be initiated remotely, allowing an attacker to potentially execute arbitrary code on the affected device. The real-world impact of this vulnerability includes the possibility of unauthorized access to the network and sensitive data, with potential targets including home users and small businesses. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update their Edimax BR-6675nD firmware to version 1.13 or later, and ensure that their device is configured to only allow incoming connections from trusted sources. Additionally, users can mitigate the risk of exploitation by disabling the PPPoE feature if it is not required for their network configuration.

items.title: CVE-2026-48831: Wine ships a .desktop file that registers itself as a MIME handler for EXE files...
items.description: Wine ships a .desktop file that registers itself as a MIME handler for EXE files and several other Windows executable file types. In some configurations, handling of an EXE file causes that file to be blindly executed with the permissions of the invoker. This allows escaping Flatpak and Snap sandboxes, because MIME handlers are not intended for use by code interpreters and loaders. NOTE: some part
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-48831 is a vulnerability in Wine that allows an attacker to execute arbitrary EXE files with the permissions of the invoker, potentially escaping Flatpak and Snap sandboxes. The vulnerability arises from Wine's registration as a MIME handler for EXE files and other Windows executable file types. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to code execution with elevated privileges, potentially affecting users of Linux systems that have Wine installed, particularly those using Flatpak or Snap packages. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update Wine to a version that does not register as a MIME handler for EXE files or use alternative compatibility layers that do not have this issue. Additionally, users can mitigate the risk by being cautious when handling EXE files and ensuring that their systems are configured to restrict the execution of unknown or untrusted files.

items.title: CVE-2026-9398: A security vulnerability has been detected in Besen BS20 EV Charging Station up ...
items.description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Besen BS20 EV Charging Station up to 20260426. This affects an unknown part of the component BLE/WiFi. Such manipulation leads to authentication bypass by capture-replay. The attack must be carried out from within the local network. Attacks of this nature are highly complex. It is indicated that the exploitability is difficult. The original disclosure
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9398 is a security vulnerability in Besen BS20 EV Charging Station up to version 20260426, affecting the BLE/WiFi component, which allows for authentication bypass via capture-replay manipulation. This vulnerability can be exploited from within the local network, but the attack complexity is high and exploitability is difficult. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow unauthorized access to the charging station, potentially disrupting its operation or allowing malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should ensure that their Besen BS20 EV Charging Station is updated to a version later than 20260426 and implement network segmentation to limit local network access. Additionally, defenders should monitor their network for suspicious activity and implement a robust authentication mechanism to prevent capture-replay attacks.

items.description: A vulnerability was found in H3C Magic B0 up to 100R002. This affects the function Edit_BasicSSID_5G of the file /goform/aspForm. Performing a manipulation of the argument param results in buffer overflow. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

items.title: Continuing Scans for swagger.json, (Wed, Jun 3rd)
items.description: Enterprise applications often still use complex standards like SOAP for web services. The big advantage of SOAP is its tight and extensive standards, which enable interoperability across an enterprise governed by web services. The disadvantage of SOAP: First, while it is de facto usually used over HTTP, it does not leverage HTTP, leading to unnecessary complexity. Secondly, kids don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t RTFM, and developers these days tend not to appreciate the art of careful system design; they rather throw code at an IDE to see what sticks, if they don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t vibe code it anyway.

items.title: Kirki, Burst Statistics WordPress Plugin Flaws in Attackers’ Crosshairs
items.description: Threat actors are exploiting vulnerable Kirki and Burst Statistics deployments to elevate privileges and take over websites. The post Kirki, Burst Statistics WordPress Plugin Flaws in Attackers’ Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: One-Click GitHub Dev Attack Lets Attackers Steal Full GitHub OAuth Tokens
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a one-click attack via Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) that makes it possible to steal a user's GitHub token. "Just by clicking a link, it's possible for an attacker to steal a GitHub token that can read and write to your repos, including private ones," security researcher Ammar Askar said. GitHub supports a feature called GitHub.dev that runs as

items.title: Hackers Target Global Stock Exchange in Espionage Operation
items.description: The attackers had access to a senior executive’s email account for 150 days and exfiltrated data for months. The post Hackers Target Global Stock Exchange in Espionage Operation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: IMA Diligence Services Data Breach Impacts 525,000 People
items.description: The affected individuals’ personal information was stolen from a legacy server managed by a third party. The post IMA Diligence Services Data Breach Impacts 525,000 People appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users
items.description: A prompt injection flaw in Google Gemini's voice assistant let attackers hide malicious commands in notifications, enabling social engineering and more.

items.title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
items.description: The Fragmented State of Modern Enterprise Identity Enterprise IAM is approaching a breaking point. As organizations scale, identity becomes increasingly fragmented across thousands of applications, decentralized teams, machine identities, and autonomous systems. The result is Identity Dark Matter: identity activity that sits outside the visibility of centralized IAM and beyond the reach of

items.title: Organizations Warned of Exploited Linux Kernel Vulnerability
items.description: An improper authentication bug allows attackers to escalate their privileges and escape containers. The post Organizations Warned of Exploited Linux Kernel Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Acer working to patch max severity zero-days in Wave 7 routers
items.description: Acer is working to address two maximum-severity zero-day vulnerabilities affecting its Wave 7 mesh routers. [...]

items.title: Beyond the Zero-Day: See Your Network Like an Attacker | Webinar with HD Moore
items.description: Assume the breach. Zero-days keep shipping, AI is writing exploits faster than anyone patches, and "patch everything in time" stopped working years ago. Stop betting the org on winning that race. You don't control which bug lands. You control what it can reach once it does. That is a question about the shape of your network, and most teams have the shape wrong. HD Moore, creator of Metasploit

items.title: ‘HTTP/2 Bomb’ Exploit Knocks Web Servers Offline in Seconds
items.description: The default HTTP/2 configuration of major web servers is vulnerable to an attack chain combining a compression bomb and a Slowloris-style hold. The post ‘HTTP/2 Bomb’ Exploit Knocks Web Servers Offline in Seconds appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Unpatched Windows Search URI Vulnerability Lets Attackers Steal NTLMv2 Hashes
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an unpatched issue that could be exploited to disclose a user's NTLMv2 hash to the attacker. Like in the case of CVE-2026-33829, which impacted the Windows Snipping Tool's ms-screensketch: URI handler, the newly flagged issue resides in the search: URI handler, per Huntress. CVE-2026-33829 refers to a spoofing vulnerability that could expose

items.title: Police dismantles 9 crime groups in illegal streaming crackdown
items.description: European and international law enforcement agencies have dismantled nine organized crime groups and arrested 29 suspects in a major crackdown on illegal streaming operations. [...]

items.title: Global Stock Exchange Hit by Monthslong Email Campaign
items.description: A threat actor got a near-continuous view into an influential finance executive's email inbox, thanks to clever use of legitimate, native Windows tools.

items.title: Microsoft Tries to Calm Legal Threat Fears After Zero-Day Disclosure Backlash
items.description: Microsoft responds to backlash over its threats of legal action against researchers who publicly disclose zero-day vulnerabilities. The post Microsoft Tries to Calm Legal Threat Fears After Zero-Day Disclosure Backlash appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google adds Android protection against AI deepfake scam calls
items.description: Google is introducing a new Android security feature that will detect and flag phone calls in which scammers use artificial intelligence to impersonate a user's personal contacts. [...]

items.title: New HTTP/2 Bomb Vulnerability Allows Remote DoS on NGINX, Apache, IIS, Envoy & Cloudflare
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a remote denial-of-service exploit that affects major web servers, including NGINX, Apache HTTPD, Microsoft IIS, Envoy, and Cloudflare Pingora. The vulnerability has been codenamed HTTP/2 Bomb by Calif. "The vulnerable behavior exists in each server's default HTTP/2 configuration," the company said, adding it was discovered by OpenAI Codex by chaining

items.title: VS Code zero-day lets hackers steal GitHub tokens in one click
items.description: A security researcher has released exploit code for a Visual Studio Code (VS Code) zero-day vulnerability that allows attackers to steal GitHub authentication tokens by tricking users into clicking a link. [...]

items.title: Weedhack Attacks Minecraft Users, CountLoader Hits 86K, Miners Spread via Pirated Content
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new campaign targeting Minecraft players via YouTube to spread malware capable of gaining control of victims' systems. The Minecraft-focused malware-as-a-service (MaaS) campaign has been codenamed Weedhack by McAfee Labs, stating the activity has been active since January 2026 and impersonates Minecraft clients and mods to infect users. In all, 3820

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Microsoft's Coreutils project brings Linux commands to Windows
items.description: Microsoft announced today at its Build 2026 developer conference the release of Coreutils for Windows, bringing many commonly used Linux command-line utilities to Windows as native applications. [...]

items.title: OpenAI upgrades GPT-5.5, as it plans to retire legacy ChatGPT models
items.description: OpenAI says it's rolling out a new update that improves the existing GPT-5.5 Instant model, and this move comes ahead of the scheduled retirement of multiple legacy models, including o3. [...]

items.title: Critical Kirki flaw exploited to hijack WordPress admin accounts
items.description: Hackers are exploiting a critical privilege escalation vulnerability (CVE-2026-8206) in the Kirki plugin for WordPress to take over any user account, including those belonging to administrators. [...]

items.title: Over 116,000 Minecraft systems infected in WeedHack malware campaign
items.description: A large-scale malware campaign dubbed WeedHack is targeting Minecraft players and has infected more than 116,000 systems since January. [...]

items.title: Zoom CISO: AI as a Security Enabler, Not Role-Replacer
items.description: Zoom CISO Sandra McLeod discusses the challenges of securing a global communication platform, the promise of AI-driven security workflows, and her advice for aspiring cybersecurity leaders.

items.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
items.description: Once targeting just Microsoft 365, the phishing-as-a-service platform now aims at AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms, while relying on device code phishing.

items.title: DriveSurge Hijacks Thousands of Sites for ClickFix, FakeUpdate Attacks
items.description: A sneaky, wide-scale IAB operation uses a malicious traffic distribution system (TDS) to redirect visitors of trusted websites to ones that deliver malware.

items.title: Trump Signs Executive Order That Invites Vetting of Top AI Models for National Security Risks
items.description: The order establishes a framework for the federal government to vet the national security risks of the most advanced AI systems for up to a month before their public release. The post Trump Signs Executive Order That Invites Vetting of Top AI Models for National Security Risks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: China Uses Dual-Method Cyberattack on Czech Orgs
items.description: China is stealing data from high-value targets via a sneaky, double-layer spear-phishing campaign that includes the Azureveil malware.

items.title: Securing AI Agents Before They Go Rogue Is Next to Impossible
items.description: High-autonomy agents with broad permissions and unfettered access are a recipe for disaster, and enterprises need to act now before they become the next horror story.

items.title: Google June 2026 Android Update Patches 124 Flaws, One Actively Exploited
items.description: Google on Monday released patches for 124 security vulnerabilities impacting its Android operating system for the month of June 2026, including one high-severity flaw in the Framework component that has come under active exploitation. Tracked as CVE-2025-48595 (CVSS score: 8.4), the security flaw has been described as a case of privilege escalation without requiring any user interaction. The

items.title: Gamaredon Exploits WinRAR to Deliver GammaWorm and GammaSteel Against Ukraine
items.description: The Russian hacking group known as Gamaredon has been attributed to the continued exploitation of a WinRAR vulnerability to deliver multiple malware families aimed at data theft and propagation. Per Sekoia, the activity involves the weaponization of CVE-2025-8088, a path traversal flaw in WinRAR, to launch an HTML Application payload dubbed GammaPhish, which is then used to retrieve an

items.title: Oracle WebLogic CVE-2024-21182 Added to KEV Catalog After Active Exploitation
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added a high-severity security flaw impacting Oracle WebLogic Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability, CVE-2024-21182 (CVSS score: 7.5), allows an unauthenticated attacker with network access to take control of susceptible servers. It was

items.title: Two New Reports Offer Competing Explanations for Cybersecurity’s Growing Crisis
items.description: As AI shortens the path from vulnerability disclosure to exploitation, researchers disagree on whether the problem is inadequate security tools or inadequate operational control. The post Two New Reports Offer Competing Explanations for Cybersecurity’s Growing Crisis appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
Beyond Assume-Breach: How AI-Native Security Will Reshape Enterprise Defense
items.description: Twenty years after Dark Reading launched, we're looking ahead at what's next for enterprise security. Spoiler: It's hyper-segmented, AI-orchestrated, and way more sophisticated than your dad's firewall.

items.title: AI-Driven Exploitation is Destroying Vulnerability Management. Here’s How to Handle It.
items.description: AI-driven exploitation timelines are rapidly shrinking, and they are not going to stop shrinking. Vulnerabilities are being discovered, reproduced, and weaponized faster than ever in the history of enterprise security. As a result, the window between a vulnerability being disclosed and indiscriminate exploitation observed across the internet is now measured in hours, not days. The industry's

items.title: New Wave Of Phishing Emails with SVG Files, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: For a few days, my SANS ISC mailbox is flooded with emails that delivers SVG files. An SVG ("Scalable Vector Graphic") is a web-friendly vector file format used for graphics and icons. No URL in the body, just â€œan imageâ€, that&#x27s the perfect way to deliver some malicious content. This isn&#x27t the first time that we see this technique used by threat actors[1].

items.title: Anthropic to Open Mythos AI to EU's ENISA
items.description: The European security agency's entry to Project Glasswing is the result of "strong bilateral cooperation" between the European Commission and Anthropic.

items.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
items.description: After a disgruntled security researcher published several zero-day exploits in recent weeks, Microsoft seemingly indicated criminal charges were in order.

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.description: Introduction

items.description: YARA-X&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s 1.17.0 release brings 5 improvements (several performance improvements) and 1 bugfix.

items.title: Analysis of a Year of Files Uploaded to DShield Sensors, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Using the data collected over the past year and using Kibana these two ES|QL query to summarize the data, this shows the list of the most uploaded threat to two DShield sensors (local and cloud) over the past year. I have sorted the activity by months that shows the evolution of files uploaded to the sensors each month. The activity peaked during the winter months (Dec 2025 - Feb 2026) and started decreasing in March 2026 for each sensor.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 28th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9948, (Thu, May 28th)

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
